evidence.scientific_clarity: Grouped bar chart directly encodes the brief's claim; OursModel visually dominates all five benchmarks, winner highlighted via color accent and bold legend label.
evidence.typography: pdf.fonttype=42 and svg.fonttype='none' set; font stack leads with Arial; PDF confirms CID TrueType Arial (subset, embedded, unicode) — no DejaVu fallback used.
evidence.palette: Okabe-Ito subset (grey/sky-blue/amber/deep-blue); no red/green for directional meaning; diagonal and dot hatching added for greyscale/B&W legibility.
evidence.layout_density: Y-axis correctly zoomed to data range (not forced to 0); however value-label offset uses max(stds[method]) rather than per-bar std, so labels sit unnecessarily high above low-std bars, wasting vertical space.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Legend placed upper-left away from tallest bars; OursModel bolded; caption lists ordering, n=5, metric, and two headline numbers — ready for submission.
evidence.vector_fidelity: pdffonts confirms GOFYPY+ArialMT and GOFYPY+Arial-BoldMT as CID TrueType, fully embedded; script runtime-checks for Type3 and exits non-zero if found — none present.
evidence.file_format: All five required artefacts present and non-trivial: figure.pdf (62 KB), figure.png (131 KB, 2109×1210 @ 300 dpi), figure.svg (178 KB), caption.tex, gen_figure.py.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption states the finding quantitatively (84.2% GSM8K, 49.3% GPQA, +12.1 pp and +12.9 pp over Baseline), explains error bars, hatching, ordering, and n=5 seeds.

overall_strengths: Exemplary font hygiene: pdf.fonttype=42 enforced, Arial embedded as CID TrueType with runtime Type3 guard — zero rasterised text in the PDF.
Caption is publication-ready: specific percentage-point gains, seed count, metric definition, and legend annotation all present.
overall_weaknesses: Value-label vertical offset uses max(stds[method]) instead of per-bar std, causing labels to float unnecessarily high above low-variance bars and wasting y-space.

No explicit single-column width test (3.5 in); at 7 in the figure targets two-column layout, but the brief mentions single-column inclusion — font sizes (6.5–9 pt) may become illegible if scaled down 50%.
